mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 400,000 signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in deteriorating labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 90 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening supply chain periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening monetary cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial unemployment claims below 300,000 signals an improving labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in improving labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3.5% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% and a 3-month decline in industrial production below 90 signals a tightening monetary cycle and a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and INDPRO < 90 for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening monetary cycle and weakening supply chain periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
